title: Odontophyllum longibracteolatum
intro: Odontophyllum longibracteolatum, also known as Atractylodes lancea, is a type of plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders. The bioactive constituents of Odontophyllum longibracteolatum include sesquiterpenes, such as atractylenolides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, as well as being used in the production of tonics and supplements to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Odontophyllum longibracteolatum is a species of plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family is part of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Loganiaceae and Gelsemiaceae. The order Gentianales is a part of the clade Asterids, a large group of flowering plants that also includes the orders Lamiales and Scrophulariales. The clade Asterids is a subgroup of the larger clade Euasterids, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the orders Ericales and Cornales. The Euasterids are a subgroup of the larger clade Angiosperms, which is the group of flowering plants.
